top_signals.title: Tungsten Can Deter China’s Military Dominance
top_signals.summary: Around 80% of the world’s tungsten, used in bullets and armor plating, is produced by China. Bloomberg Opinion columnist David Fickling went to a remote mine in Australia to learn why that’s about to change. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: ECB publishes indicative operational calendars for 2028
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: ECB publishes indicative operational calendars for 2027
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: USMC awards $20 million contract for first autonomous ground vehicles
top_signals.summary: The Marine Corps is pursuing a first-of-its-kind $20 million production contract for fully autonomous ground vehicles.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Statistical Notice 2026/06 - Form PL - updates to definitions
top_signals.summary: Statistical Notices update the definitions and guidance contained in the Banking Statistics Yellow Folder
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: MetaMask launches stablecoin yield account with card spending
top_signals.summary: MetaMask launches Money Account it says offers up to 4% variable APY on mUSD stablecoin balances and card spending, with DeFi-powered yield via vaults, excluding the UK and sanctioned jurisdictions.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Microsoft adds smarter bot protection to Teams meetings
top_signals.summary: Microsoft has introduced a new Teams admin policy that allows organizers to prevent third-party bots from joining meetings without approval. [...]
